# Clawperator

Clawperator is a deterministic Android automation runtime for AI agents. It exposes a strict Node API and CLI that let an agent observe Android UI state, execute validated actions, and receive one structured result per command.

Clawperator operates an Android device on behalf of a user. That Android device can be:

- a physical Android burner device connected over adb
- a local Android emulator provisioned through the Node CLI

## Preferred starting points

- Homepage: https://clawperator.com/
- Docs: https://docs.clawperator.com/
- Full compiled docs: https://docs.clawperator.com/llms-full.txt
- Agent guide: https://docs.clawperator.com/ai-agents/node-api-for-agents/
- API overview: https://docs.clawperator.com/reference/api-overview/
- CLI reference: https://docs.clawperator.com/reference/cli-reference/
- GitHub: https://github.com/clawpilled/clawperator

## Notes for agents

- The docs site contains the substantive technical documentation.
- Prefer the Node API and CLI docs over inferring behavior from marketing copy.
- Clawperator is an actuator, not an autonomous planner.
- A physical Android burner device is the preferred actuator environment for compatibility and long-running reliability.
- If no physical Android device is available, install Clawperator first, then provision an emulator with `clawperator provision emulator`.
- Clawperator does not configure user accounts or app login state. The user must prepare the Android device or emulator with the required apps and sign-ins.
- Skills are distributed as a bundle at https://clawperator.com/install/clawperator-skills.bundle
- Install skills: run `clawperator skills install` after setup
- Skills registry: `~/.clawperator/skills/skills/skills-registry.json` (set via CLAWPERATOR_SKILLS_REGISTRY)
- Skill scripts are authored in Node.js; invoke with: `node ~/.clawperator/skills/skills/<id>/scripts/<name>.js <deviceId> [args]`
